records.ety: [Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to place side by side, or parallel: cf. F. paralllisme.]

defs: The quality or state of being parallel.

quotes: A close parallelism of thought and incident.

defs: Similarity of construction or meaning of clauses placed side by side, especially clauses expressing the same sentiment with slight modifications, as is common in Hebrew poetry; e. g.: -- At her feet he bowed, he fell: Where he bowed, there he fell down dead. Judg. v. 27.
quotes: At her feet he bowed, he fell: Where he bowed, there he fell down dead.
